pinterest.title: The Accidental Discovery of Penicillin: Revolutionizing Medicine and Saving Millions
pinterest.description: In 1928, bacteriologist Alexander Fleming noticed a mold (Penicillium notatum) contaminating a petri dish had killed the surrounding bacteria. This serendipitous observation led to the discovery of penicillin, the world's first widely used antibiotic. https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/item/accidental-discovery-penicillin #surfaced #discoveries

posts.title: Your Body Emits a Faint, Continuous Light That's Invisible to Our Eyes

pinterest.title: Your Body Emits a Faint, Continuous Light That's Invisible to Our Eyes
pinterest.description: Despite appearing dark, the human body constantly emits a faint amount of visible light, a phenomenon known as biophoton emission. This glow is thousands of times weaker than what our eyes can detect, but advanced sensitive cameras have captured it, often peaking in the late afternoon. https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/item/human-bioluminescence-invisible-glow #surfaced #discoveries

pinterest.title: There Are More Stars in the Universe Than Grains of Sand on Earth
pinterest.description: Astronomers estimate there are roughly 200 sextillion stars in the observable universe (2 x 10^23).
